text: Risk management is an important part of running any project or business. The first step is identifying risks, which can be anything from financial issues to technical problems. Once risks are found, teams create mitigation strategies to reduce their impact, such as using safer materials or improving training. However, not all risks can be avoided, so contingency plans are necessary. These are backup actions put in place for if something goes wrong. Finally, the process must include monitoring, which means regularly checking risks and plans to adapt to new situations. By following these steps, individuals and organisations can handle uncertainty with confidence.
